source_notes: CRITICAL VERIFICATION FLAG: WebSearch/WebFetch tools were not loadable in this scout session, so all data is sourced from training knowledge through January 2026 with reasoned projection forward to late April 2026. Today's date (2026-04-29) is ~4 months past knowledge cutoff. Items requiring verification before betting use: (1) all current rankings and rank_last_week (clay swing reshuffles WTA/ATP top-50 every week through Madrid/Rome), (2) YTD 2026 W-L and titles after January (Australian Open results known; Sunshine Double and clay swing partially projected), (3) recent_results from March-April 2026 (Indian Wells, Miami, Monte Carlo, Barcelona, Madrid), (4) coach changes after Jan 2026, (5) any injuries/withdrawals after Jan 2026. Solid: player demographics (name, country, DOB-derived age, height, hand), career-high ranks achieved before 2026, stylistic strengths/weaknesses, surface preferences. Madrid Open 2026 is in progress through this date — QF/SF results listed as 'projected/TBD' where uncertain. Rankings ordered by best estimate of ATP/WTA live ranking on 2026-04-28 Monday release.

players.name: Jannik Sinner
weaknesses: second-serve hold rate dips on clay vs top-10
occasional drop in first-serve % under pressure

players.name: Carlos Alcaraz
strengths: best forehand under pressure on tour
weaknesses: serve speed below elite on second serve

players.name: Taylor Fritz
strengths: one of best flat forehands on tour

players.name: Daniil Medvedev
strengths: deepest return position on tour, neutralizes serves

players.name: Novak Djokovic
strengths: best return game in tennis history

players.name: Andrey Rublev
weaknesses: mental fragility in deciding sets, well-documented
no plan B when forehand misfires

players.name: Holger Rune
strengths: forehand power off both wings on clay

players.name: Ben Shelton
strengths: lefty serve top-3 in mph and disguise

players.name: Ugo Humbert
strengths: lefty flat forehand on fast hard

players.name: Tommy Paul
weaknesses: shorter players' typical reach issues vs big servers

players.name: Matteo Berrettini
strengths: forehand and serve combo elite when healthy

players.name: Iga Swiatek
strengths: forehand topspin best on WTA clay
weaknesses: faster surfaces flatten her topspin advantage

players.name: Jasmine Paolini
strengths: smile keeps her loose under pressure

players.name: Jessica Pegula
weaknesses: QF curse at slams broken but recurring

players.name: Leylah Fernandez
strengths: clutch in tight moments (US Open run heritage)

players.name: Naomi Osaka
strengths: serve when on, top-3 mph WTA

players.name: Sofia Kenin
strengths: clutch baseline tactics on big points

players.name: Tatjana Maria
strengths: slice off both wings rare on WTA
weaknesses: lacks pace weapon vs power players
